description: Current rollout maturity. It tells whether the app is still in pilot, generally available, or paused.
Commercial packaging status. It captures whether pricing is usage based, seat based, internal only, or grandfathered legacy pricing.
Risk status from legal, privacy, and security review. This field tells whether rollout is cleared, under review, or actively blocked.
Executive owner sentiment. Green means healthy, amber means caution, red means serious concern or likely escalation.
Operational quality of the app's technical integrations. Stable means reliable, flaky means intermittent issues, degraded means currently underperforming.
Likelihood that leadership or cross-functional teams will need to intervene soon.
Commercial agreement status. This can indicate an active contract, a pending renewal, or a deal that needs executive signoff before it can move.
Support intensity committed to the app, ranging from standard coverage to white glove support.
Primary geographic restriction for data hosting and processing, such as global, EU only, or US only.
How strongly the product team is backing the app on the roadmap. It may be committed, exploratory, or frozen.
